mappings.rationale: Directly covers the study of peoples and cultures worldwide, including ethnographic approaches to diverse societies across regions.
Provides methods for understanding material cultures of peoples across the world regions listed in this section.
Directly covers peoples and cultures of North America including sub-Arctic, Southwest, Plains, and eastern woodland groups.
Covers the cultures of Mesoamerica, a key region under the Middle America subsection.
Covers Maya civilization in Mesoamerica and Central America, directly relevant to peoples of Middle America.
Covers the encounter between European and indigenous cultures in the Americas, relevant to understanding cultural transformation in Middle and South America.
Covers Celtic peoples of the Atlantic fringe and European plain, directly relevant to peoples of Europe.
Covers Norse peoples and their culture across northern Europe and the Atlantic fringe.
Covers the cultural interactions among peoples of Central Asia, East Asia, the Middle East, and Europe along trade routes.
Covers the Mongol peoples of Central Asia and their interactions with cultures across Eurasia.
Covers peoples and cultures of sub-Saharan Africa including the western and eastern Sudan, Guinea coast, Congo, and southern Africa.
Covers religious dimensions of African cultures across the sub-Saharan regions outlined in this section.
Covers Chinese culture and society, directly relevant to peoples and cultures of East Asia.
Covers Indian culture and society, directly relevant to peoples and cultures of South Asia.
Covers Japanese culture and society, directly relevant to peoples and cultures of East Asia.
Directly covers the peoples and cultures of the Southeast Asian region.
Covers Iranian peoples and culture, directly relevant to the Middle East and North Africa subsection on Iran.
Covers Korean culture and society, relevant to peoples and cultures of East Asia.
Covers Pakistani culture and society, relevant to peoples of South Asia.
Covers Australian peoples and cultures, directly relevant to the Oceania subsection on Australia.
Covers Canadian peoples and cultures including indigenous communities, relevant to North America.
Covers Brazilian culture and society, relevant to peoples and cultures of South America.
Covers French culture and society, relevant to peoples of Europe along the Atlantic fringe and Mediterranean.
Covers Italian culture and society, relevant to peoples of Europe along the Mediterranean.
Covers Scottish peoples and culture, relevant to the Atlantic fringe of Europe.
Directly covers the Arctic region and its peoples, matching the first subsection on Arctic cultures.
Studies musical cultures of peoples worldwide, providing a cross-cultural perspective on the diverse regions covered.
Covers the culture of ancient Egypt in northeastern Africa, relevant to peoples of the Middle East and North Africa.
Covers cultures of the ancient Mashriq region including Mesopotamia, relevant to Middle East peoples.
Covers Assyrian civilization in the Mashriq, relevant to peoples and cultures of the Middle East.
Covers Babylonian civilization in the Middle East, relevant to peoples of the Mashriq region.
Covers Greek peoples and culture along the Mediterranean, relevant to European peoples.
Covers the Etruscan people of the Italian peninsula, relevant to Mediterranean European cultures.
Covers the dispersal and cultural maintenance of peoples across world regions, a cross-cutting theme for this section.
Covers the cultural transformation of Mediterranean and Middle Eastern peoples in the transitional period.
Covers the earliest human cultures across world regions before written records.
Covers the reassertion of indigenous cultures during the end of colonial rule across Africa, Asia, and Oceania.
Covers the culture and society of the Spartans, a distinctive people of Mediterranean Europe.
Covers the distinctive peoples and cultures of the southeastern United States.
Covers peoples and cultures of the western regions of North America including the Plains, Great Basin, and Southwest.
Covers a major African American cultural movement, relevant to understanding North American peoples and cultures.
Covers literary traditions of North American indigenous peoples, providing cultural insight into their worldviews.
Covers the samurai as a cultural institution of East Asian (Japanese) society.
Covers a foundational cultural and philosophical tradition shaping East Asian peoples and societies.
Covers a key cultural and religious tradition of Central Asian and South Asian peoples.
Covers the Druidic tradition of Celtic peoples on the Atlantic fringe and European plain.
Covers the diverse peoples and cultures of Central and Eastern Europe under Habsburg rule.
Covers the culture and peoples of the Byzantine world at the crossroads of Europe and the Middle East.
Covers Rastafari as a Caribbean cultural and religious movement, relevant to peoples of the Caribbean.
Covers the dispersal and culture of Jewish peoples across multiple world regions.
Covers Asian diaspora cultures in North America, relevant to understanding multicultural peoples of the region.
Covers African American peoples and their cultural development in North America.
Covers cultural expressions of peoples in Middle and South America during the colonial period.
Covers the encounters between European explorers and indigenous peoples across all world regions.
Covers the cultural experience of segregated communities, relevant to understanding ethnic enclaves across world regions.
Covers the coexistence and interaction of diverse peoples and cultures within societies.
Examines the cultural legacy of colonialism on peoples across Africa, Asia, and the Americas.
Covers the diverse peoples and cultures of Central Europe under the Holy Roman Empire.
Covers a major cultural form of South Asian peoples, reflecting diverse Indian cultures.
Covers the political dimensions of sub-Saharan African peoples and their societal organization.
Covers the cultures and peoples of the Mashriq region in the Middle East.
Covers how global interconnection affects peoples and cultures across all world regions.
Covers the origins and dispersal of human populations across the world regions listed in this section.
Covers the peoples and cultures of northern Mexico and Mesoamerica during a transformative period.
Covers the peoples and cultures of Russia spanning Siberia and the European plain.
Covers the cultural interactions between Anatolian and Greek peoples in the Mediterranean and Middle East.
